meta.description: Continuous, evidence-graded feature backlog for the multi-region momentum platform. Every item is critiqued by all four roles (see ../roles.md) and carries acceptance criteria + a rollout plan. Generated with the four-lens process in ../PROCESS.md; validated by ../validate.py.

items.title: Point-in-time constituents ingestion (kill survivorship bias)
items.problem: Default universes are today's liquid names, so every reported CAGR/Sharpe is a survivorship-inflated upper bound and cannot back a real paper->live capital decision.
items.hypothesis: Running the backtest on real point-in-time index membership (plus delisted prices) lowers reported CAGR by a material, believable margin and makes the numbers trustworthy.

evidence.claim: The PIT mechanism (--point-in-time, MembershipTable, members_asof) is already built; only real membership/delisted files are missing.
Backtesting on today's index vs point-in-time constituents inflates returns ~1-4%/yr, worse for high-turnover indices.
Omitting delisting returns biases upward; the missing returns are sharply negative (-30% NYSE/AMEX, -55% Nasdaq).

product_owner.notes: The acknowledged 'big one'. Done means the README survivorship caveat is deleted, not hedged.
product_owner.concern: Norgate/CRSP data cost and FTSE/ASX delisted-price completeness.

data_scientist.notes: Quantify the static-vs-PIT gap; require delisted coverage and effective-date (not announcement-date) membership.

chief_engineer.notes: Plugs into constituents.py/regions.py/data.py; config_knob rollout. Keep synthetic membership clearly test-only.
chief_engineer.concern: Symbology mismatch (LSE/ASX renames) vs the data.py loader.

algo_trader.notes: Backtest is fiction until real US/FTSE membership lands; block sizing off non-PIT Sharpe.
algo_trader.concern: PIT and non-PIT numbers must diverge sanely before I size a dollar.

acceptance_criteria.statement: run_backtest --point-in-time runs on real membership files for all three regions.
acceptance_criteria.metric: 3/3 regions have a non-synthetic constituents_file loaded

acceptance_criteria.statement: The survivorship-bias magnitude is quantified static vs PIT.
acceptance_criteria.metric: CAGR delta (static - PIT) >= 1.5pp, reported in output

acceptance_criteria.statement: PIT snapshots reconcile against official index history.
acceptance_criteria.metric: >= 99% of month-end memberships match

acceptance_criteria.statement: Delisted-name terminal returns are represented, not silently dropped.
acceptance_criteria.metric: >= 95% of index turnover events covered or Shumway replacement return applied (see F13)

stages.name: synthetic
stages.exit_criteria: members_asof never returns a future member (boundary tests green)

stages.name: full-live
stages.scope: all three regions on real files
stages.exit_criteria: AC1-AC4 all met; README caveat removed

guardrail_metrics.threshold: PIT file covers >= 90% of the current universe or warns

rollout.rollback: Omit --point-in-time; loader falls back to the static universe with the existing survivorship banner. No state migration.

items.title: Deflated Sharpe + PBO overfitting gate for the equity sleeves
items.problem: The equity sweep reports raw Sharpe with no correction for the number of configurations tried, so a curve-fit parameter set can look like a real edge and reach the book.
items.hypothesis: Porting the FX Deflated-Sharpe/PBO machinery to the equity side lets us reject parameter sets that only clear raw Sharpe, catching overfitting before promotion.

evidence.claim: The FX subsystem already implements Deflated Sharpe and PBO in pure region-agnostic NumPy, directly importable.
The bias note is explicit: count trials and deflate for them (DSR + PBO) or you manufacture an edge.
The equity sweep exists (grid over TOP_N x lookback) and already produces the trial set DSR needs.

product_owner.notes: Stops 'beat the index by 2%' being manufactured overfitting; FX proves the pattern so it is a port.
product_owner.concern: A passing gate could be mistaken for proof of a live edge.

data_scientist.notes: n_trials must equal the real grid size; PSR(0) net of costs > 0.95; watch overlapping-window autocorrelation.
data_scientist.concern: A small equity grid undercounts n_trials and inflates DSR.

chief_engineer.notes: validation.py is region-agnostic; mirror test_fx_validation.py as tests/test_equity_validation.py. S effort.
chief_engineer.concern: Passing DSR could be treated as false confidence (invariant #5 spirit).

algo_trader.notes: Cheap given the plumbing exists; the only thing stopping curve-fit params reaching my book.

acceptance_criteria.statement: Every reported equity backtest surfaces DSR and a PBO verdict alongside Sharpe.
acceptance_criteria.metric: run_backtest + report.py + dashboard BACKTEST tab all show DSR & PBO

acceptance_criteria.statement: The trial count fed to DSR equals the actual sweep grid size.
acceptance_criteria.metric: n_trials == len(sweep grid) asserted in test

acceptance_criteria.statement: A parameter set that fails the gate is flagged and not promoted.
acceptance_criteria.metric: PBO < 0.5 (target < 0.2) and DSR > 0.95 required to pass

stages.name: synthetic
stages.exit_criteria: DSR/PBO match FX reference values on shared fixtures

stages.name: single-sleeve
stages.exit_criteria: gate produces a stable verdict across two runs

guardrail_metrics.threshold: no backtest metric shown without DSR context

rollout.rollback: Feature is additive reporting; remove the --deflate flag output. No behaviour change to signals or sizing.

items.title: Live-vs-backtest tracking and P&L attribution
items.problem: Nobody can tell whether the paper book's P&L resembles the backtest that justified the strategy, so the 'paper-trade before risking capital' step has no pass/fail signal.
items.hypothesis: Logging realized paper fills vs backtest-predicted fills per rebalance and attributing the gap (selection vs costs vs FX) makes divergence measurable and gate-able.

evidence.claim: No live-vs-backtest comparison exists today in paper or live.
The bias literature says to expect live < backtest and to haircut the Sharpe.
The FX book already logs an equity curve that this can borrow its pattern from.

product_owner.notes: Closes the loop the README caveat implies; must compare like-for-like params, not a moving backtest baseline.
product_owner.concern: Short paper history gives noisy early comparisons.

data_scientist.notes: Target annualized tracking error <= 200bps; >=80% of divergence explained by known buckets over >=6 months.
data_scientist.concern: A cost bucket could mask a real signal bug.

chief_engineer.notes: Attribution must call strategy.compute_targets on the same eligible set paper saw, no hindsight refetch. Golden-run test.
chief_engineer.concern: Risks invariant #1 and #3 if it rebuilds weights independently.

algo_trader.notes: Most important missing piece; daily tracking-error alert, kill if divergence > slippage budget for 3 rebalances.
algo_trader.concern: Without it I cannot trust the paper P&L at all.

acceptance_criteria.statement: A view shows realized vs backtest-predicted CAGR/Sharpe/turnover per sleeve since inception.
Divergence is attributed to selection vs execution vs cost vs FX buckets.
acceptance_criteria.metric: >= 80% of cumulative divergence explained by named buckets

acceptance_criteria.statement: A golden-run test proves paper weights equal backtest weights on a fixed-seed synthetic run.
acceptance_criteria.metric: paper weights == backtest weights bit-for-bit (extends tests/test_consistency.py)

acceptance_criteria.statement: Tracking error is bounded and alertable.
acceptance_criteria.metric: annualized tracking error <= 200bps or alert

stages.name: shadow
stages.scope: log expected vs realized fills without surfacing

rollout.rollback: Reporting-only module; disable the tab. No change to trading state.

items.title: Cross-border allocation rebalancing in the paper sim
items.problem: The paper sim funds each sleeve once and never rebalances, so it drifts from the equal-third target that the backtest assumes, making the two diverge structurally.
items.hypothesis: Periodically transferring cash between regional books toward target allocation (charging the same FX spread the backtest models) keeps paper and backtest comparable without hurting risk-adjusted return.

evidence.claim: config.ALLOCATION_REBALANCE and FX_SPREAD_BPS exist but are used only by portfolio_backtest.py, never paper_trade.py.
Cross-border rebalancing in the paper sim is listed as an open roadmap item.

product_owner.notes: Keeps paper capital at target as sleeves drift; opt-in and documented since some users prefer fund-once treasury.
product_owner.concern: Adds real FX drag to paper P&L, changing headline numbers.

data_scientist.notes: Only ship if net Sharpe improvement >= 0 after FX_SPREAD_BPS cost; trigger at >= 5pp drift.
data_scientist.concern: FX spread can erode the diversification benefit.

chief_engineer.notes: Charge the spread on every transfer exactly like portfolio_backtest.py; keep transfers in the portfolio layer, not per-sleeve accounting.
chief_engineer.concern: Risks invariant #6 (mixing currencies inside a sleeve) and #2 if the spread is skipped.

algo_trader.notes: Treasury/ops decision, not an edge decision; fund-once is fine until drift exceeds ~10pp for a quarter.

acceptance_criteria.statement: Paper sim periodically transfers cash toward target allocation, charging FX spread.
acceptance_criteria.metric: combined equity conserved except for spread cost (test)

acceptance_criteria.statement: Rebalance cadence honours config.ALLOCATION_REBALANCE and is opt-in.
acceptance_criteria.metric: default off; cadence asserted in tests/test_paper_trade.py

acceptance_criteria.statement: Rebalancing does not fire while a sleeve is halted by the drawdown breaker.
acceptance_criteria.metric: no transfer during an active halt (test)

stages.name: paper
stages.exit_criteria: no unexplained equity drift over a quarter

guardrail_metrics.threshold: cumulative FX cost logged and matches transfers

rollout.rollback: Set config.PAPER_ALLOCATION_REBALANCE=False; reverts to funded-once. No state migration.

items.title: Static monthly HTML/PDF tearsheet reports
items.problem: There is no shareable dated performance snapshot outside the live interactive dashboard.
items.hypothesis: A scheduled monthly tearsheet (equity curve, CAGR/Sharpe/MaxDD, sleeve breakdown) dropped as an artifact gives a distributable record without a manual step.

evidence.claim: Static reports are an explicitly open roadmap item; the interactive dashboard covers the live view.
report.py already renders markdown metrics that a tearsheet can extend.

product_owner.notes: Much of the gap is already closed by the self-contained dashboard export; value is mostly distribution.
product_owner.concern: Redundant unless it adds real distribution value; PDF adds a dependency to a zero-dep stack.

data_scientist.notes: No new statistical claim; must reconcile exactly with computed metrics and never omit DSR/PBO context.
data_scientist.concern: A stale tearsheet during an active halt misleads.

chief_engineer.notes: Small; reuse portfolio_markdown's SYNTHETIC banner and ship as a GH Actions artifact on a monthly cron.
chief_engineer.concern: Must never drop the synthetic disclaimer (invariant #5).

algo_trader.notes: Reporting only, nothing to act on faster than the terminal UI; investor-letter theatre for the desk.

acceptance_criteria.statement: A monthly cron emits a dated tearsheet with equity curve and headline metrics per sleeve.
Tearsheet numbers reconcile with already-computed metrics and carry the synthetic/PIT disclaimer.
acceptance_criteria.metric: 0-tolerance reconciliation vs metrics.py; disclaimer present

acceptance_criteria.statement: Generation is fast enough to run alongside the engine.
acceptance_criteria.metric: runtime < 30s

rollout.rollback: Remove the cron step / --tearsheet flag. Additive; nothing to revert.

items.title: Transaction-cost upgrade: market impact + borrow costs
items.problem: Costs are a flat bps + commission floor; this understates cost for illiquid names and models no market impact, and there is no borrow cost path for a future short book.
items.hypothesis: A volume-scaled (square-root) impact term keyed off ADV, plus a borrow path that only activates if shorting is added, gives more honest net returns as size scales.

evidence.claim: Costs are modelled as flat slippage bps + commission floor; data.py fetches only Close, so ADV/volume is not ingested.
High-turnover edges die on realistic costs; impact matters as AUM grows.
The strategy is long-only today, so borrow cost is currently a no-op.

product_owner.notes: Borrow is moot (long-only); impact only matters past retail scale. Revisit when a short book or larger AUM arrives.
product_owner.concern: Effort spent modelling a cost we don't incur yet.

data_scientist.notes: Only justify if impact changes turnover-heavy names' cost by >= 20% vs flat bps and Sharpe degradation < 0.3.
data_scientist.concern: Overfitting impact params to sparse volume data adds noise.

chief_engineer.notes: L effort: needs a volume/ADV data-layer addition. Unify cost calc into one fees.py function used by both engines.
chief_engineer.concern: Two divergent cost paths today (paper inline vs backtest round_trip) risk invariant #3.

algo_trader.notes: Real but modest ROI at current AUM/turnover; matters once size scales. Reject/resize orders whose modelled impact exceeds a bps threshold vs ADV.
algo_trader.concern: Calibrate against real IBKR fills for a quarter first.

acceptance_criteria.statement: Default-off, the new model reproduces the old flat-cost numbers exactly.
acceptance_criteria.metric: regression test: identical metrics with impact model disabled

acceptance_criteria.statement: Impact cost is reported separately from flat cost.
acceptance_criteria.metric: backtest output + report.py show an impact line

acceptance_criteria.statement: Both engines share one cost function.
acceptance_criteria.metric: paper_trade and backtest call the same fees.py entrypoint (consistency test)

stages.name: synthetic
stages.exit_criteria: monotone in size; AC1 regression holds

rollout.rollback: Set config.IMPACT_MODEL='flat'; restores current behaviour exactly. No migration.

items.title: Automated data-quality gate before signals run
items.problem: Signals run on whatever the feed returns; a stale, gapped, or split-corrupted print (a known yfinance failure mode) can silently corrupt every downstream number and generate a real order.
items.hypothesis: A shared validator that flags stale prices, gaps, outliers and corporate-action jumps before signals — and holds prior weights rather than trade on garbage — prevents bad prints from becoming orders.

evidence.claim: yfinance split/dividend errors and LSE pence scaling are called out as known landmines.
Look-ahead / bad-data is the most easily introduced backtest error; remedy is disciplined data checks.
No data-quality module exists today; data.py loads and returns prices directly.

product_owner.notes: Do first: cheap, no external data dependency, and a bad price silently corrupts every other item.
product_owner.concern: Overly strict thresholds could drop legitimate volatile names.

data_scientist.notes: Flag single-day return > 50% (US/ASX) or > 30% (FTSE pence) unless matched to a corp action; false-positive rate < 5%.
data_scientist.concern: Without a split/dividend calendar, false positives dominate.

chief_engineer.notes: ONE shared validator called identically by backtest and paper; any repair uses only trailing data.
chief_engineer.concern: Two divergent implementations would break invariants #1 and #3.

algo_trader.notes: Hard block on rebalance for any stale/duplicate/impossible price; hold prior weights and log the reason.
algo_trader.concern: Must run before every paper/live rebalance, not just in backtest.

acceptance_criteria.statement: A single validator flags stale prices, gaps, outliers and corp-action jumps and is called by both backtest and paper_trade.
acceptance_criteria.metric: one module; consistency test asserts both engines call it

acceptance_criteria.statement: Impossible single-day moves are caught region-aware.
acceptance_criteria.metric: return > 50% (US/ASX) / > 30% (FTSE) flagged unless matched corp action; false-positive < 5%

acceptance_criteria.statement: Staleness excludes a ticker.
acceptance_criteria.metric: 0 price change > 5 consecutive trading days -> excluded

acceptance_criteria.statement: On a rebalance, flagged names hold prior weight and are logged.
acceptance_criteria.metric: no order generated for a flagged ticker (test); reason logged

stages.name: synthetic
stages.scope: injected NaN gaps / jumps / stale rows
stages.exit_criteria: flagged tickers excluded consistently in both engines (AC1)

rollout.rollback: Set config.DATA_QUALITY_GATE=False; signals run on raw data as today. No migration.

items.title: Purged & embargoed walk-forward CV for the equity sleeves
items.problem: The equity sweep only checks a flat-vs-peaky surface; naive walk-forward on overlapping momentum windows leaks the future across fold boundaries, inflating confidence.
items.hypothesis: Porting the FX purge/embargo logic (embargo >= one rebalance period) gives the equity side leakage-free CV splits and honest per-fold performance to feed the F2 gate.

evidence.claim: The FX subsystem has purged+embargoed walk-forward (walk_forward_predict, embargo, min_train).
Naive k-fold on time series leaks the future; purge and embargo to prevent it.
The 200-day trend/regime MA can leak across a boundary if the embargo is shorter than its lookback.

product_owner.notes: sweep.py alone doesn't remove overfitting; this gives PBO real CV splits and is a prerequisite for a meaningful F2.
product_owner.concern: Monthly rebalance + limited history leaves few independent splits.

data_scientist.notes: Embargo >= 21 trading days (one rebalance); n_folds >= 6; compute out-of-fold DSR/PBO per fold.
data_scientist.concern: Monthly cadence gives wide per-fold Sharpe CIs.

chief_engineer.notes: The equity signal is a fixed formula, not a trained model, so walk_forward_predict's fit/predict doesn't map cleanly; may overlap F2's PBO.
chief_engineer.concern: Defer if it only duplicates F2; risks invariant #1 only if misapplied.

algo_trader.notes: Research hygiene: a param set surviving only non-purged CV should be down-weighted in sizing confidence.
algo_trader.concern: Moves the needle on trust, not raw P&L.

acceptance_criteria.statement: Equity CV uses purge + embargo of at least one rebalance period.
acceptance_criteria.metric: embargo >= 21 trading days; purge covers the 12m momentum + 200d MA lookback

acceptance_criteria.statement: Out-of-fold DSR/PBO are reported per fold.
acceptance_criteria.metric: n_folds >= 6, min_train >= 60 months

acceptance_criteria.statement: Purge/embargo boundaries are unit-tested.
acceptance_criteria.metric: tests mirror tests/test_fx_walkforward.py

stages.name: synthetic
stages.scope: boundary tests on synthetic monthly series

guardrail_metrics.threshold: purged vs naive CV Sharpe gap reported, not hidden

rollout.rollback: Drop --purged-cv; sweep reverts to the flat-surface check. Additive.

items.title: Momentum-crash / factor-crowding exposure monitor
items.problem: The only crash defence is the reactive dual 200-day trend/regime filter; there is no early warning when correlation/crowding spikes ahead of a classic momentum-crash setup.
items.hypothesis: A crowding/crash monitor (rolling cross-sectional correlation, vol spikes, the down-then-rebound setup) flags elevated tail risk before the regime filter trips, giving a human a chance to review.

evidence.claim: Momentum crashes are the known tail risk of this strategy; the dual 200MA filter is the tested primary defence.
Daniel & Moskowitz (2016): crashes cluster after market drawdown-then-rebound; conditional beta/skew is predictive.
config already caps selection correlation (avg_correlation ~0.6); a monitor can watch realized correlation vs that cap.

product_owner.notes: Adds visibility on top of the mechanical defence; lowest urgency of the yes-items since the regime filter is the tested primary.
product_owner.concern: Crowding metrics are noisy and prone to false alarms.

data_scientist.notes: Flag 60-day realized vol > 2x trailing 3yr avg; flag the index >10% below 200MA AND +5% past-month rally; alert if held pairwise correlation > 0.7.
data_scientist.concern: May be redundant with the trend filter; crowding wants external factor data.

chief_engineer.notes: Observability-only first (dashboard alert). If ever wired into sizing it MUST flow through strategy.compute_targets.
chief_engineer.concern: A paper-only bolt-on the backtest lacks would break invariant #3.

algo_trader.notes: Alert, don't auto-liquidate; human reviews before the regime filter would even trip.
algo_trader.concern: False alarms in genuine V-shaped recoveries cause costly de-risking.

acceptance_criteria.statement: A crowding/crash metric is surfaced with an elevated-risk flag, not just after a drawdown.
acceptance_criteria.metric: dashboard/report shows the metric + threshold flag

acceptance_criteria.statement: It is observability-only and does not alter sizing.
acceptance_criteria.metric: no change to compute_targets output (consistency test)

acceptance_criteria.statement: It fires on a replayed momentum-crash scenario.
acceptance_criteria.metric: synthetic crash window triggers the flag

rollout.rollback: Remove the tile/module. Pure observability; nothing to revert.

items.title: Paper->live promotion gate (Decision agent) + min-account-size check
items.problem: Switching from paper to real capital is an unguarded manual call; nothing objectively checks track record, overfitting, drawdown, or account solvency before a live order can fire.
items.hypothesis: A hard promotion gate in the execution path — refusing live unless track-record, DSR/PBO, drawdown and min-account-size criteria are met — operationalizes the HANDOFF 'Decision agent' and protects capital.

evidence.claim: HANDOFF describes a Decision agent that should gate execution, including a minimum-account-size pre-trade check.
config has a MIN_VIABLE_EQUITY_BASE solvency floor and MAX_DRAWDOWN_STOP but no promotion gate; execution has no gate check.
The $1k micro account already demonstrated fee drag kills small size.

product_owner.notes: One command answers 'is this account ready to go live?' against explicit criteria before dry_run=False.
product_owner.concern: Criteria could be loosened under pressure to go live.

data_scientist.notes: Require >= 6 monthly rebalances, live Sharpe within 1 SE of backtest-predicted, equity >= 20x commission floor. Note 6 points is underpowered.
data_scientist.concern: Conflating the solvency floor with a promotion-readiness gate under-specifies risk.

chief_engineer.notes: Hard-block in execution_ibkr.rebalance() at LIVE_PORT unless criteria met; explicit override flag for human bypass; CI gate. S effort.
chief_engineer.concern: Protects invariant #4 (whole-share/floor) against underfunded live accounts.

algo_trader.notes: Non-negotiable before real IBKR capital: N months paper + tracking error inside budget (F3) + min-size per region.
algo_trader.concern: Nothing goes live without it, full stop.

acceptance_criteria.statement: execution rebalance() refuses a live (dry_run=False) order unless every promotion criterion is met.
acceptance_criteria.metric: rebalance() raises at LIVE_PORT when criteria unmet (unit test)

acceptance_criteria.statement: Criteria include track record, DSR/PBO pass, drawdown bound and min account size per region.
acceptance_criteria.metric: >= 6 rebalances; equity >= 20x commission floor; DSR>0.95 & PBO<0.5

acceptance_criteria.statement: A human override is explicit and audited.
acceptance_criteria.metric: override flag required; decision appended to an audit log

stages.name: synthetic
stages.exit_criteria: gate raises on fail, passes on qualifying (AC1)

stages.name: canary-live
stages.exit_criteria: gate blocks until AC2 fully met

rollout.rollback: Set config.PROMOTION_GATE=False only with explicit sign-off; default remains on. Reverting restores the current (unguarded) manual call.

items.title: Execution-quality TCA logging (implementation shortfall)
items.problem: No trade records decision price vs fill price, so realized slippage is never measured against the modelled slippage_bps and the backtest-vs-live gap can't be diagnosed.
items.hypothesis: Capturing fills and logging implementation shortfall per order — rolled up per sleeve vs the modelled cost — reveals where live underperforms the backtest and recalibrates the cost model.

evidence.claim: execution_ibkr.py places orders and discards the result; no fill is captured today.
The bias literature says expect live < backtest and measure the haircut.
Modelled slippage is a flat per-region bps (ASX 10 / US 5 / FTSE 8) with no realized comparison.

product_owner.notes: Measures the exact 'expect live < backtest' haircut; feeds F6 calibration and F3 attribution.
product_owner.concern: Paper sim has no real impact, so paper TCA is only a proxy.

data_scientist.notes: Alert if realized slippage exceeds modelled by > 50% over 20 trades; avg quarterly IS <= modelled cost.
data_scientist.concern: Cross-currency IS must convert to AUD correctly or it misattributes by region.

chief_engineer.notes: Requires first capturing the ib_insync Trade/fill objects; pure measurement, no invariant risk.

algo_trader.notes: Where the backtest-vs-live gap actually gets diagnosed; build together with F3.
algo_trader.concern: Small-cap ASX spreads not captured by flat 10bps understate true shortfall.

acceptance_criteria.statement: Every fill logs decision price, fill price and computes implementation shortfall.
acceptance_criteria.metric: IS logged per order (mocked-fill test)

acceptance_criteria.statement: Realized vs modelled slippage is rolled up per sleeve in AUD.
acceptance_criteria.metric: per-region realized-vs-modelled bps report

acceptance_criteria.statement: An alert fires when realized systematically exceeds modelled.
acceptance_criteria.metric: > 50% over a rolling 20 trades -> alert

stages.name: shadow
stages.scope: capture fills, compute IS, don't alert

guardrail_metrics.threshold: IS aggregated in AUD, not mixed

rollout.rollback: Disable tca.py logging. Measurement-only; no trading behaviour change.

items.title: Drawdown circuit-breaker telemetry and alerting
items.problem: The drawdown breaker and min-viable-size gate exist but only print to a log; since the engine runs unattended via cron, a halt could go unnoticed for weeks.
items.hypothesis: Firing a notification on the breaker's halt/resume transition makes an unattended halt actionable within one trading day instead of silent.

evidence.claim: config defines MAX_DRAWDOWN_STOP and a cooldown; the breaker prints to stdout in paper_trade.py with no notification channel.
The engine is designed to run unattended in the background / via cron.

product_owner.notes: Ships immediately, no data/research dependency; a silent breaker is worse than none.
product_owner.concern: Alert fatigue if thresholds too sensitive.

data_scientist.notes: The breaker logic is already implemented/tested; alerting is fine but the fixed 25%/21d threshold isn't per-region validated (FX uses per-profile 0.12-0.30).
data_scientist.concern: Telemetry doesn't re-validate whether 25%/21d is sound.

chief_engineer.notes: Fire only on the False->True halt transition (and resume) to avoid spam. Pure observability.
chief_engineer.concern: None; also surface halt state on the dashboard OVERVIEW tab.

algo_trader.notes: Push/email the instant the breaker trips; a silent circuit breaker is the actual gap.
algo_trader.concern: Wire it before any live capital.

acceptance_criteria.statement: A halt or resume transition (or min-viable-size trip) pushes a notification.
acceptance_criteria.metric: notify() called exactly once per transition (unit test)

acceptance_criteria.statement: Alerts do not repeat every day while halted.
acceptance_criteria.metric: 0 duplicate alerts across consecutive halted runs (test)

acceptance_criteria.statement: Alert latency fits the engine cadence.
acceptance_criteria.metric: <= 1 trading day from breach to notification

stages.name: paper
stages.exit_criteria: real transition delivers within a day (AC3)

rollout.rollback: Unset config.BREAKER_ALERT_CHANNEL; breaker still halts as today, just without notifying.

items.title: Delisting-return correction (Shumway -30%/-55%)
items.problem: Even with point-in-time membership, a dropped constituent whose terminal price series is missing contributes no (sharply negative) delisting return, leaving residual upward bias.
items.hypothesis: Applying a Shumway replacement return (-30% NYSE/AMEX, -55% Nasdaq) when a delisted name has no matched terminal price closes the residual survivorship gap left by F1.

evidence.claim: constituents.py provides PIT membership but has no terminal-return correction for delisted names with missing prices.
Delisting bias is big enough to flip conclusions; correct with -30%/-55% replacement returns.

product_owner.notes: Directly completes F1; small once membership data exists.
product_owner.concern: Only meaningful after F1 lands real membership files.

data_scientist.notes: Apply the region-appropriate replacement return; re-run F1's CAGR-delta acceptance to confirm the correction moves the number.

chief_engineer.notes: A pure addition inside the constituents/backtest join; config knob for the replacement rates.
chief_engineer.concern: Must apply only at the delisting date (no lookahead), invariant #1.

algo_trader.notes: Makes the survivorship fix honest; without it F1 still overstates.
algo_trader.concern: Low P&L impact but needed for number integrity.

acceptance_criteria.statement: A delisted name with no matched terminal price gets a region-appropriate replacement return.
acceptance_criteria.metric: -30% (NYSE/AMEX) / -55% (Nasdaq) applied at the delisting date

acceptance_criteria.statement: The correction is applied causally.
acceptance_criteria.metric: no lookahead test regression

acceptance_criteria.statement: The correction measurably lowers reported CAGR vs uncorrected PIT.
acceptance_criteria.metric: CAGR(uncorrected) - CAGR(corrected) > 0

stages.name: synthetic
stages.scope: fixture with a delisted, price-missing name
stages.exit_criteria: replacement return applied at the right date (AC1/AC2)

stages.name: single-sleeve
stages.exit_criteria: AC3 CAGR moves in the expected direction

rollout.rollback: Disable the replacement-return knob; behaviour reverts to F1-only PIT. Additive.

items.title: Market-data provider fallback / redundancy
items.problem: Yahoo 403 is repeatedly called out as blocking real backtests and CI; a single-source feed means the scheduled engine and CI can silently go stale on an outage.
items.hypothesis: A secondary source (e.g. stooq / cached snapshots) behind the loader keeps the engine and CI running when the primary feed fails, without changing prices when it succeeds.

evidence.claim: Yahoo 403 blocking real data is flagged in three places as a known operational risk.
data.py has a single primary loader path plus synthetic_region for offline tests.

product_owner.notes: Stops the scheduled engine and CI going silently stale; operational resilience.

data_scientist.notes: Fallback prices must reconcile with the primary within tolerance or the switch itself introduces a data break.
data_scientist.concern: Provider disagreement on adjusted prices / corp actions.

chief_engineer.notes: Lazily-imported secondary loader behind the existing interface; must pass the F7 data-quality gate on either source.
chief_engineer.concern: Keep to one loader interface (no divergent paths).

algo_trader.notes: A dead feed on rebalance day is a real risk; a fallback beats trading on nothing.
algo_trader.concern: Never silently trade off a source that disagrees materially with the primary.

acceptance_criteria.statement: On primary-feed failure the loader falls back to a secondary source behind the same interface.
acceptance_criteria.metric: simulated 403 -> secondary path returns prices (test)

acceptance_criteria.statement: Fallback data passes the F7 data-quality gate before use.
acceptance_criteria.metric: gate runs on either source

acceptance_criteria.statement: A provider switch is logged and reconciles within tolerance.
acceptance_criteria.metric: overlap disagreement < 1% or warn

rollout.rollback: Unset config.DATA_FALLBACK_SOURCE; loader uses the primary only, as today.

items.title: Pre-trade ADV / liquidity cap
items.problem: compute_targets has no liquidity check; a capped single-name weight in a thin FTSE/ASX name can be multiple days of that name's volume, which no pre-trade control catches.
items.hypothesis: Capping each order at a fraction of trailing ADV before it reaches execution prevents positions the desk can't realistically enter or exit.

evidence.claim: Selection/sizing has no ADV/liquidity constraint; weights are inverse-vol capped only.
ASX/FTSE sleeves include thinner names where a capped weight can exceed daily volume.

product_owner.notes: Prevents un-enterable positions; a real desk control.
product_owner.concern: Needs ADV data the loader doesn't fetch yet (shares the F6 volume dependency).

data_scientist.notes: Cap at e.g. <= 10% of trailing 20-day ADV; measure how often it binds and its return drag.
data_scientist.concern: Sparse volume data on thin names makes ADV noisy.

chief_engineer.notes: If it changes sizing it MUST run inside strategy.compute_targets so backtest and paper agree.
chief_engineer.concern: A paper-only cap the backtest lacks breaks invariant #3.

algo_trader.notes: Cap order size as % of trailing ADV before it ever reaches execution_ibkr.
algo_trader.concern: Without it a thin-name target is days of volume.

acceptance_criteria.statement: Each target is capped at a configurable fraction of trailing ADV inside compute_targets.
acceptance_criteria.metric: no target > cap * ADV; cap default 10% of 20-day ADV

acceptance_criteria.statement: Backtest and paper apply the identical cap.
acceptance_criteria.metric: consistency test asserts both route through compute_targets

acceptance_criteria.statement: How often the cap binds and its return drag are reported.
acceptance_criteria.metric: bind-rate and CAGR delta logged

stages.name: synthetic
stages.exit_criteria: cap binds and lowers the target (AC1)

rollout.rollback: Set config.ADV_CAP_PCT=None; sizing reverts to uncapped inverse-vol. No migration.

items.title: CI backtest regression gate
items.problem: CI runs only pytest; nothing checks that a refactor silently changed strategy behaviour (accidental lookahead, cost regression, sizing drift).
items.hypothesis: A CI step running run_backtest --synthetic and asserting Sharpe/CAGR/MaxDD/turnover stay within tolerance of a committed baseline catches behavioural regressions before merge.

evidence.claim: CI currently runs only `pytest -q`, with no behavioural backtest check.
Invariant #1/#2 (no-lookahead, costs-on) are exactly the kind of property a refactor can silently break.

product_owner.notes: Protects the invariants at merge time; cheap safety net.
product_owner.concern: Baseline must be updated deliberately, not rubber-stamped.

data_scientist.notes: Use synthetic (invariant #5) with a fixed seed; tolerance tight enough to catch a lookahead leak but not flaky.
data_scientist.concern: Synthetic metrics are pipeline checks, never performance claims.

chief_engineer.notes: run_backtest --synthetic vs a committed baseline JSON; fail the build on out-of-tolerance drift. S effort.

algo_trader.notes: Stops a quiet behaviour change reaching the book via a refactor.
algo_trader.concern: Moves the needle on trust, not P&L.

acceptance_criteria.statement: CI runs a synthetic backtest and compares to a committed baseline.
acceptance_criteria.metric: Sharpe/CAGR/MaxDD/turnover within +/- tolerance of baseline JSON

acceptance_criteria.statement: The build fails on out-of-tolerance drift.
acceptance_criteria.metric: non-zero exit on regression

acceptance_criteria.statement: Baseline updates are explicit and reviewed.
acceptance_criteria.metric: baseline change requires a committed file diff

rollout.rollback: Mark the CI step non-blocking (continue-on-error). Tests still run.

items.title: Structured run manifests + experiment ledger
items.problem: No run records the git commit, StrategyParams, region set, or data date-range; sweep/tune trials aren't persisted, so trial counts (the input to DSR) and reproducibility are lost.
items.hypothesis: Emitting a JSON manifest per run and appending sweep/tune trials to a versioned ledger gives reproducibility, an audit trail, and the honest trial count the F2 gate needs.

evidence.claim: Neither backtest.py, paper_trade.py nor report.py record a run manifest today.
The #1 process safeguard is to log your trial count and deflate for it.

product_owner.notes: Reproducibility + the missing input to F2/F8; feeds F3 attribution and F10's promotion audit.
product_owner.concern: Only valuable if actually consulted, not write-only.

data_scientist.notes: Persist params, data vintage, metrics, DSR/PBO per run; the ledger IS the trial count.
data_scientist.concern: Trial count must be honest (include abandoned runs).

chief_engineer.notes: Emit a JSON manifest (git SHA, params, region set, date range) per run; small, additive.
chief_engineer.concern: Keep manifests out of trading state files.

algo_trader.notes: Audit trail for what actually ran before capital moved.

acceptance_criteria.statement: Every backtest/paper run emits a JSON manifest.
acceptance_criteria.metric: manifest includes git SHA, params hash, region set, data date-range

acceptance_criteria.statement: Sweep/tune trials append to a versioned ledger.
acceptance_criteria.metric: trial count readable back for DSR n_trials

acceptance_criteria.statement: Manifests validate against a schema.
acceptance_criteria.metric: manifest schema check green (reuse product/validate.py pattern)

stages.name: synthetic
stages.scope: manifest written on a synthetic run

rollout.rollback: Manifests are additive side-files; stop writing them. No behaviour change.

items.title: Schema-validated paper/FX state files
items.problem: paper_state_{account}.json and fx book state are plain JSON with no schema check on load; a crashed mid-write or a manual edit can silently corrupt live trading state.
items.hypothesis: Validating state shape and types on load/save (and refusing to trade on an invalid file) prevents corrupted state from driving real orders and underpins the F10 promotion gate.

evidence.claim: Paper state is persisted as plain JSON with no schema validation on load/save.
F10's promotion gate needs trustworthy state before it can judge readiness.

product_owner.notes: Prevents silent state corruption reaching live; prerequisite-grade for F10.
product_owner.concern: Schema must version cleanly as fields evolve.

data_scientist.notes: Low research value but real integrity value; ensure a rejected file fails loudly rather than resetting equity.
data_scientist.concern: A too-strict schema could brick a recoverable file.

chief_engineer.notes: Validate shape/types in load_state/save_state; refuse to trade on an invalid file. Reuses the product schema pattern nicely.
chief_engineer.concern: None; add a migration path for old files.

algo_trader.notes: A corrupted state file driving orders is a nightmare; block it.
algo_trader.concern: Must fail safe (halt), not silently reinitialize.

acceptance_criteria.statement: State files are validated against a schema on load and save.
acceptance_criteria.metric: invalid file -> load raises, no trade (test)

acceptance_criteria.statement: A rejected file fails safe (halt), never silently resets equity.
acceptance_criteria.metric: no equity reset on invalid state (test)

acceptance_criteria.statement: A migration path handles pre-schema files.
acceptance_criteria.metric: old-format fixture migrates or is quarantined, not lost

stages.name: shadow
stages.exit_criteria: 0 false rejections on existing state files

rollout.rollback: Set config.VALIDATE_STATE_FILES=False; load behaves as today (no validation).

items.title: Sharpe haircut for expected live performance
items.problem: Backtest Sharpe is reported raw, which oversells expected live performance to stakeholders who don't see the multiple-testing penalty.
items.hypothesis: Reporting a haircut-adjusted expected Sharpe (from the F2 DSR / multiple-testing penalty) next to the raw backtest Sharpe sets honest expectations that F3 can later validate against realized live results.

evidence.claim: The bias note says expect live < backtest and haircut the Sharpe.
DSR already quantifies the deflation the haircut expresses (F2 provides it).

product_owner.notes: Stops overselling the backtest; a one-line honesty upgrade to reporting.
product_owner.concern: Stakeholders may still anchor on the raw number.

data_scientist.notes: Derive the haircut from the DSR/multiple-testing penalty; later compare to realized live Sharpe via F3.
data_scientist.concern: The haircut is an estimate, not a guarantee.

chief_engineer.notes: Trivial once F2 exists: a derived column in report.py/dashboard. XS effort.

algo_trader.notes: I size off the haircut number, not the raw one.

acceptance_criteria.statement: Reports show a haircut-adjusted expected Sharpe next to raw backtest Sharpe.
acceptance_criteria.metric: both values present in report.py + dashboard

acceptance_criteria.statement: The haircut is derived from the DSR / trial-count penalty.
acceptance_criteria.metric: haircut = f(DSR, n_trials); unit test

acceptance_criteria.statement: The haircut can be reconciled against realized live Sharpe over time.

stages.name: synthetic
stages.scope: unit test on the haircut formula

stages.name: full-live
stages.exit_criteria: AC1 met: both values shown in every report

guardrail_metrics.threshold: raw Sharpe never shown without the haircut

rollout.rollback: Remove the derived column. Additive reporting only.
